name: Rice and Curry
shortDescription: Sri Lanka's iconic meal with fragrant rice and assorted curries.
description: A quintessential Sri Lankan meal featuring steamed white rice served with a rich chicken curry, dhal curry, pol sambol, papadam, and a seasonal vegetable side. Every bite is a burst of authentic island flavours cooked with freshly ground spices and coconut milk.

reviews.author: Kasun P.
reviews.comment: Nothing beats a good Sri Lankan rice and curry!

reviews.author: Nishani S.
reviews.comment: Just like my grandmother used to make. Absolutely perfect.

name: Kottu Roti
shortDescription: Street-style chopped roti stir-fried with egg, vegetables, and spices.
description: Sri Lanka's most beloved street food — godamba roti chopped and stir-fried on a hot iron griddle with egg, carrot, leek, onion, and a special blend of spices. Available with chicken or fully vegetarian. The rhythmic sound of blades chopping the roti is music to the ears.

reviews.author: Dilshan R.
reviews.comment: Best kottu I've had in Colombo!

reviews.author: Amaya K.
reviews.comment: The spice level is just right. Totally addictive.

name: Egg Hoppers
shortDescription: Bowl-shaped crispy rice flour pancakes with a soft egg centre.
description: Traditional Sri Lankan hoppers (appam) made from fermented rice flour and coconut milk batter, cooked in a small wok until the edges are lacy and crispy while the base stays soft. Cracked with a fresh egg inside. Best enjoyed with pol sambol and seeni sambol.

reviews.author: Priya W.
reviews.comment: Perfect crispy edges with a soft centre — morning staple!

reviews.author: Roshan M.
reviews.comment: Great with pol sambol on the side.

name: String Hoppers
shortDescription: Delicate steamed rice noodle nests served with coconut sambol.
description: Fine noodle nests pressed from rice flour dough and steamed to perfection. Soft, light, and mildly flavoured, string hoppers are a staple Sri Lankan breakfast and dinner. Served with a generous helping of pol sambol and a bowl of warm chicken or dhal curry for dipping.

reviews.author: Tharini J.
reviews.comment: Authentic and soft — reminded me of home!

reviews.author: Saman B.
reviews.comment: Simple, light, and comforting.

name: Fish Ambul Thiyal
shortDescription: Dry sour fish curry made with goraka and bold island spices.
description: A signature Sri Lankan dry fish curry from the southern coast, made with thick cuts of tuna cooked in a robust paste of goraka (gamboge), black pepper, garlic, and cinnamon. The intense sourness and smoky depth make it utterly unique. Best served with steamed white rice.

reviews.author: Nimali C.
reviews.comment: Absolutely authentic southern taste. Loved every bite!

reviews.author: Ruwan T.
reviews.comment: The goraka sourness is perfectly balanced. A true Sri Lankan classic.

name: Devilled Chicken
shortDescription: Crispy fried chicken tossed in a fiery sweet-sour devilled sauce.
description: Tender chicken pieces fried until golden then tossed in a bold sauce of capsicum, onion, tomato ketchup, soy sauce, vinegar, and chilli. Sri Lanka's favourite dry-style chicken dish — sweet, sour, and gloriously spicy. A must-order at any local restaurant.

reviews.author: Lahiru A.
reviews.comment: Perfectly spicy with a great sauce. Best devilled chicken in town!

reviews.author: Sanduni F.
reviews.comment: I order this every single time I visit.

name: Prawn Curry
shortDescription: Juicy prawns slow-cooked in creamy coconut milk curry.
description: Fresh tiger prawns simmered in a rich Sri Lankan curry gravy made with coconut milk, tomatoes, onion, mustard seeds, curry leaves, and fragrant spices. Creamy, aromatic, and deeply satisfying. Pair it with steamed rice or a warm roti for the full experience.

reviews.author: Chamari D.
reviews.comment: The coconut milk gravy is divine. Prawns were perfectly cooked.

reviews.author: Isuru P.
reviews.comment: Rich and creamy — restaurant quality at a fair price.

name: Dhal Curry
shortDescription: Slow-cooked red lentils in a golden coconut milk base.
description: A beloved everyday Sri Lankan staple — red lentils cooked until perfectly tender in a fragrant broth of coconut milk, turmeric, green chilli, mustard seeds, and curry leaves. Finished with a temper of red onion and dried chilli. Comforting, nutritious, and utterly delicious.

reviews.author: Savini R.
reviews.comment: Exactly like the dhal curry my mother makes. Wonderful!

reviews.author: Harith N.
reviews.comment: Simple but incredibly flavourful.

name: Lamprais
shortDescription: Dutch-Burgher heritage: rice, curries and sambol baked in banana leaf.
description: A cherished Dutch-Burgher heirloom dish — fragrant Dutch rice, a seeni sambol, a prawn blachan, two curries (chicken and dhal), and a slice of frikkadel, all wrapped in a banana leaf and baked until the flavours meld together in perfect harmony. A true Sri Lankan celebration meal.

reviews.author: Genevieve P.
reviews.comment: Absolutely extraordinary. This is Sri Lanka's finest dish.

reviews.author: Bernard F.
reviews.comment: The banana leaf wrapping adds such a unique aroma. Exceptional!

name: Pittu
shortDescription: Steamed rice flour and coconut cylinders, a beloved Lankan classic.
description: Pittu is a traditional Sri Lankan dish made of alternating layers of rice flour and freshly grated coconut, packed into a cylindrical bamboo or metal mould and steamed. Light yet filling, pittu pairs perfectly with coconut milk, chicken curry, or a spicy katta sambol.

reviews.author: Malsha K.
reviews.comment: Perfect pittu — soft, fluffy, and just right with coconut milk.

reviews.author: Bhanuka L.
reviews.comment: Classic Sri Lankan breakfast. Always a great choice.

name: Wattalappam
shortDescription: Silky Malay-origin coconut custard pudding with jaggery.
description: A beloved Sri Lankan dessert of Malay origin — a rich, dense steamed custard made with coconut milk, jaggery (kithul treacle), eggs, and an aromatic blend of cardamom, cloves, and nutmeg. Deep caramel in colour and intensely flavourful. A festive favourite across all communities.

reviews.author: Fathima Z.
reviews.comment: The most authentic wattalappam I've tasted. Rich and aromatic!

reviews.author: Lakshmi V.
reviews.comment: Silky smooth with a deep jaggery flavour. Absolutely divine.

name: Kiri Bath
shortDescription: Auspicious milk rice cooked in thick coconut milk.
description: Kiri Bath is Sri Lanka's most auspicious dish — white rice slow-cooked in thick coconut milk until it becomes a rich, creamy, and slightly dense cake. Traditionally cut into diamond or square shapes and served at New Year celebrations and special occasions. Best enjoyed with pol sambol or lunu miris.

reviews.author: Anoma P.
reviews.comment: Perfectly creamy kiri bath — brings back childhood New Year memories.

reviews.author: Ruwanthi S.
reviews.comment: Rich and satisfying. A wonderful traditional dish.

name: Pol Roti
shortDescription: Thick coconut flatbread cooked on a griddle — a Sri Lankan staple.
description: Rustic and filling flatbread made from wheat flour, freshly grated coconut, green chilli, and onion, cooked on a dry hot griddle until slightly charred. Pol roti is a common Sri Lankan breakfast or dinner paired beautifully with pol sambol, dhal curry, or a fried egg.

reviews.author: Dilini W.
reviews.comment: Crispy outside, soft inside — just how roti should be!

reviews.author: Ajith K.
reviews.comment: Perfect with dhal curry. A truly comforting meal.

name: Buffalo Curd with Treacle
shortDescription: Creamy buffalo curd drizzled with sweet kithul palm treacle.
description: Sri Lanka's most iconic natural dessert — thick, velvety buffalo curd with a pleasantly tart flavour, generously drizzled with dark, sweet kithul palm treacle. A simple yet extraordinarily delicious combination that has been enjoyed across the island for centuries. Often served chilled in an earthen clay pot.

reviews.author: Shehan M.
reviews.comment: The treacle sweetness with the tangy curd is simply divine.

reviews.author: Oshadi R.
reviews.comment: Served beautifully in a clay pot. Tasted incredible!

name: Crab Curry
shortDescription: Whole blue swimmer crab in a bold coconut milk masala.
description: A showstopper Sri Lankan seafood dish featuring whole blue swimmer crabs cooked in a thick, aromatic masala of coconut milk, roasted spices, tomato, and green chilli. The sauce is deep, complex, and finger-licking good. Best eaten with your hands alongside steamed rice or pol roti.

reviews.author: Vimukthi N.
reviews.comment: Absolutely worth every rupee. The sauce alone is a masterpiece!

reviews.author: Thilini P.
reviews.comment: Best crab curry on the island. Mind-blowing flavour.

name: Isso Vadai
shortDescription: Crispy lentil fritters topped with whole fresh prawns.
description: A beloved Sri Lankan street snack from Galle Face Green — golden-fried masala vadai (lentil fritters) topped with whole fresh prawns pressed in before frying. Crunchy on the outside, soft on the inside, and bursting with seasoning. Best eaten hot with a squeeze of lime.

reviews.author: Akila C.
reviews.comment: These transport me straight to Galle Face! Perfectly crispy.

reviews.author: Hasini F.
reviews.comment: The prawn on top is the best part. Incredible street food!

name: Wood Apple Juice
shortDescription: Tangy-sweet fresh wood apple drink — a Sri Lankan favourite.
description: A refreshing and nutritious traditional Sri Lankan drink made by blending the unique wood apple (beli fruit) pulp with coconut milk and a touch of jaggery. Earthy, tangy, creamy, and mildly sweet all at once. A wonderfully exotic flavour profile that is distinctly Sri Lankan.

reviews.author: Naduni M.
reviews.comment: Tastes exactly like what you get at the village kade. Love it!

reviews.author: Kelum J.
reviews.comment: Sweet, tangy, and very refreshing on a hot day.

name: Ceylon Tea
shortDescription: Iconic Sri Lankan black tea served strong with milk.
description: Sri Lanka's most famous export — a perfectly brewed cup of world-renowned Ceylon black tea sourced from the hill country estates of Nuwara Eliya or Uva. Served strong with rich milk and your choice of sweetener. Bold, malty, and deeply aromatic — the ultimate Sri Lankan experience in a cup.

reviews.author: Asanka W.
reviews.comment: Nothing like a proper cup of Ceylon tea. World class!

reviews.author: Renuka L.
reviews.comment: Strong, fragrant, and full-bodied. The best tea I've had.

name: King Coconut Drink
shortDescription: Fresh chilled thambili — Sri Lanka's natural energy drink.
description: Sri Lanka's golden king coconut (thambili) is cracked open fresh and served chilled, offering a naturally sweet, electrolyte-rich coconut water unlike any other in the world. Known for its medicinal properties, it is the perfect thirst-quencher in Sri Lanka's tropical heat. Pure, natural, and utterly refreshing.

reviews.author: Chanuka B.
reviews.comment: Nothing more refreshing than a fresh thambili on a hot afternoon!

reviews.author: Shyama P.
reviews.comment: Natural, sweet, and the best drink in Sri Lanka hands down.

name: Wambatu Moju
shortDescription: Sri Lankan pickled eggplant in a sweet-sour-spicy glaze.
description: A vibrant and tangy Sri Lankan condiment dish — deep-fried slices of eggplant (brinjal) tossed in a glossy glaze of coconut oil, vinegar, sugar, mustard seeds, turmeric, and dried chilli. Sweet, sour, and spicy, wambatu moju is the perfect accompaniment to any rice and curry meal.

reviews.author: Roshani D.
reviews.comment: Perfectly balanced sweet and sour. Goes great with rice!

reviews.author: Manoj T.
reviews.comment: A flavourful side dish. Makes any rice and curry meal complete.

name: Chicken Masal Kothu
shortDescription: Spicy masala chicken kothu with chopped roti and bold island spices.
description: A chef's special take on Sri Lanka's favourite street food — godamba roti chopped on a hot griddle with tender masala chicken, leek, carrot, onion, egg, and a fiery blend of roasted spices. Rich, smoky, and packed with flavour in every bite.

reviews.author: Tharindu M.
reviews.comment: The masala chicken makes this kothu next level. Absolutely incredible!

reviews.author: Priya N.
reviews.comment: Best kothu on the menu — spicy, smoky, and perfectly chopped.

name: Veg Full Meal
shortDescription: Complete vegetarian rice and curry platter with seasonal sides.
description: A wholesome Sri Lankan vegetarian feast — steamed rice served with dhal curry, jackfruit curry, beetroot mallung, brinjal moju, pol sambol, papadam, and a seasonal vegetable curry. Balanced, nourishing, and bursting with authentic home-style flavours.

reviews.author: Anjali R.
reviews.comment: So many flavours on one plate. A perfect veg meal!

reviews.author: Kavindu S.
reviews.comment: Generous portions and every curry tastes homemade.

name: Schezwan Rice
shortDescription: Wok-tossed rice with schezwan sauce, vegetables, and chilli heat.
description: Fragrant basmati rice stir-fried in a blazing hot wok with schezwan sauce, bell peppers, spring onion, carrot, and green chilli. Bold, tangy, and fiery — a fusion favourite with a Sri Lankan kick. Finished with a drizzle of sesame oil for extra aroma.

reviews.author: Dinesh K.
reviews.comment: Perfect spice level and so much flavour in every spoonful!

reviews.author: Madhavi L.
reviews.comment: Great fusion dish — tangy, spicy, and very satisfying.

name: Mongolion Fried Rice
shortDescription: Savory wok-fried rice with vegetables and Mongolion-style seasoning.
description: Classic Mongolion-style fried rice — fluffy grains wok-tossed with tender chicken, egg, spring onion, capsicum, and a savoury soy-garlic glaze. Smoky from the high heat, lightly sweet, and deeply umami. A crowd-pleasing chef's pick.

reviews.author: Ruwan P.
reviews.comment: Smoky, savoury, and perfectly seasoned. My go-to fried rice!

reviews.author: Ishara W.
reviews.comment: The wok flavour really comes through. Delicious every time.

name: Sea Food String Hopper Kothu
shortDescription: String hoppers chopped with fresh seafood and aromatic spices.
description: An innovative chef's creation — delicate string hoppers chopped and stir-fried on the griddle with prawns, cuttlefish, crab meat, leek, onion, and a rich seafood masala. A luxurious twist on the classic kothu with the light texture of idiyappam and bold ocean flavours.

reviews.author: Nimal J.
reviews.comment: The seafood mix is incredible — unique and absolutely worth trying!

reviews.author: Chathuri F.
reviews.comment: String hopper kothu is genius. Fresh seafood and perfect spice.

name: Full Grilled Chicken
shortDescription: Whole chicken marinated in spices and flame-grilled to perfection.
description: A show-stopping whole chicken marinated overnight in Sri Lankan spices, yoghurt, and lime, then slow-grilled over open flame until the skin is crisp and golden while the meat stays juicy and tender. Served with pol sambol, lime wedges, and a side of fries. Perfect for sharing.

reviews.author: Hasitha G.
reviews.comment: Juicy inside, crispy outside. The best grilled chicken I've had!

reviews.author: Melani D.
reviews.comment: Perfect for sharing — huge portion and incredible flavour.
